---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 8a32a95c24be180511c0c46d8ef66f9b7b83fcf3
findings:
  - { id: judged-ac2-coverage-predicate-vl020-parity, kind: judged, text: "ac-2's frozen text asserts coverage 'is decided by the same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies' and that a present-but-malformed file is merely 'never mistaken for coverage'. The implementation (scaffoldMissingObligations / evidence.ObligationKindAt) is deliberately STRICTER than VL-020's existence-only check: it decodes the file, requires for_kind==kind, and on a malformed file at the convention path or a decodable-but-for_kind-mismatched file it REFUSES accept (exit 2) rather than treating the pair as uncovered-and-scaffolding it. So the predicate is not parity with VL-020 (which passes on both those states), and 'never mistaken for coverage' understates the actual behavior (accept aborts). This is fully self-disclosed in the ac-2 body's round-3/round-4 correction notes and stated as queued for ratification / an accepted deviation — flagged here only so the frozen-text-vs-code gap stays tracked until the AC wording is ratified; it is a safety-increasing (refuse-where-VL-020-would-pass) divergence, never a coverage-weakening one. (confidence 0.40)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "The VL-020-parity deviation, re-slugged from the build round's judged-backstop-coverage-vs-vl020-predicate (accepted-deviation). Behavior is correct and deliberately STRICTER than VL-020 (adds for_kind==kind; catches at accept-time a misfile VL-011 reds at lint, one gate earlier). The frozen ac-2 'same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies' names the shared convention-path locus, not full parity; the body's round-3/round-4 corrections disclose it exactly. Frozen-text precision QUEUED FOR RATIFICATION (08-revision-notes at feature close). Honesty slice, not fix-class." }
not-resurfaced:
  - { id: judged-backstop-coverage-vs-vl020-predicate, kind: judged, text: "ac-2's frozen wording asserts coverage is decided by 'the same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies (a DECODABLE obligation.md at the convention path)'. The code diverges: VL-020 (vl020.go:74-77) is existence-only — it keys existingObligations on d.Kind=='obligation' at the RelPath and never inspects for_kind (its own doc: 'Existence only ... does not re-validate that file's own content'). The backstop (acceptobligation.go:127-147 via evidence.ObligationKindAt, which decodes for_kind) additionally requires present \u0026\u0026 forKind==kind, and REFUSES accept (exit 2) in two states VL-020 treats as covered/passing: (1) a present-but-undecodable file at the convention path, and (2) a decodable obligation at the exact convention path whose for_kind disagrees with its filename (VL-020 passes; it is VL-011, not VL-020, that reds this). So the predicate is provably STRICTER than VL-020, not 'the same', and it introduces refusal outcomes ac-2's scaffold-missing/skip-covered/byte-untouched framing does not contemplate. This is no longer a silent divergence: the spec body's round-3 and round-4 corrections now name it exactly (naming judged-coverage-predicate-forkind-keying, retracting the earlier 'can only refuse where VL-020 would pass' and 'VL-020 reds it' claims), and the code comments disclose it — the residual gap is that the FROZEN ac-2 text still overclaims full predicate-parity and is only 'queued for ratification precision'. Impact is narrow (the stricter arms can only refuse safely, and end-to-end accept still converges via VL-011/VL-020 at lint), so this is disclosed-as-accepted rather than a correctness defect, but the implementation's intent is deliberately stricter than the literal AC and the two are not equivalent as ac-2 states. (confidence 0.32)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Behavior is correct and deliberately STRICTER than VL-020 (adds for_kind==kind), which is safer: it catches at accept-time a misfiled/inconsistent obligation that VL-020's existence-only check would pass and that VL-011 independently reds at lint — one gate earlier, never freezing a story with an absent or internally-inconsistent obligation. The body disclosure now names the divergence exactly (round-3 + round-4 corrections, judge-acknowledged: 'no longer a silent divergence ... disclosed-as-accepted rather than a correctness defect'; confidence fell 0.50-\u003e0.42-\u003e0.32 as it converged). The sole residual is that the FROZEN ac-2 letter ('the same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies') overclaims full parity when the backstop is stricter; frozen AC text cannot be edited in place (X-16), so its precision is QUEUED FOR RATIFICATION (08-revision-notes at feature close), like init-wizard's ENOTEMPTY/os.Rename corrections. Terminal disposition per the loop policy — a disclosed-accepted honesty slice, not a fix-class finding." }
digest: sha256:daa4f4df483b7c2de2df2abbc8f24b56c33e6bfef84daa994d3d003365b9a705
integrity: sha256:818593d3496b924030b8aa319cf48c6efd07d4c004b5e42272ab5c976f9a4435
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "I've read the spec's five acceptance criteria and traced each against the implementation: `cmd/verdi/accept.go` (preFlipHead ordering, scoped `addPaths`, rollback wiring), `cmd/verdi/acceptobligation.go` (the backstop core + unlink), `cmd/verdi/acceptrollback.go`, `cmd/verdi/obligation.go` (the author verb + frozen probe), the shared seam in `internal/evidence/obligations.go`, and the board caller in `internal/workbench/obligationauthor.go`.\n\nThe implementation is closely aligned. ac-1 (preFlipHead computed before scaffold + lint gate, identical stamp, scaffolds joined to scoped `addPaths`), ac-3 (record-then-restore rollback + `unlinkScaffoldedObligations` including parent-dir residue), ac-4 (single exported render/write seam, board caller re-pointed, no second impl in cmd/verdi), and ac-5 (three-way frozen probe via merge-base, fail-open when unresolvable, exit-2 naming the path) all hold semantically.\n\nThe only genuine implementation-vs-frozen-text divergence is on ac-2's coverage predicate — and it is already extensively self-disclosed in the spec body (two round corrections) and marked \"queued for ratification,\" recorded as an accepted deviation.\n\n```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"ac2-coverage-predicate-vl020-parity\",\"text\":\"ac-2's frozen text asserts coverage 'is decided by the same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies' and that a present-but-malformed file is merely 'never mistaken for coverage'. The implementation (scaffoldMissingObligations / evidence.ObligationKindAt) is deliberately STRICTER than VL-020's existence-only check: it decodes the file, requires for_kind==kind, and on a malformed file at the convention path or a decodable-but-for_kind-mismatched file it REFUSES accept (exit 2) rather than treating the pair as uncovered-and-scaffolding it. So the predicate is not parity with VL-020 (which passes on both those states), and 'never mistaken for coverage' understates the actual behavior (accept aborts). This is fully self-disclosed in the ac-2 body's round-3/round-4 correction notes and stated as queued for ratification / an accepted deviation — flagged here only so the frozen-text-vs-code gap stays tracked until the AC wording is ratified; it is a safety-increasing (refuse-where-VL-020-would-pass) divergence, never a coverage-weakening one.\",\"confidence\":0.4}]}\n```" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-22, commit: 8a32a95c24be180511c0c46d8ef66f9b7b83fcf3 }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/obligation-seam@8a32a95c24be180511c0c46d8ef66f9b7b83fcf3, spec/obligation-seam@67a8643f30fc797295cd1c66245c9ec523d54ec9], digest: sha256:daa4f4df483b7c2de2df2abbc8f24b56c33e6bfef84daa994d3d003365b9a705, integrity: sha256:818593d3496b924030b8aa319cf48c6efd07d4c004b5e42272ab5c976f9a4435, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-ac2-coverage-predicate-vl020-parity** [accepted-deviation]: ac-2's frozen text asserts coverage 'is decided by the same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies' and that a present-but-malformed file is merely 'never mistaken for coverage'. The implementation (scaffoldMissingObligations / evidence.ObligationKindAt) is deliberately STRICTER than VL-020's existence-only check: it decodes the file, requires for_kind==kind, and on a malformed file at the convention path or a decodable-but-for_kind-mismatched file it REFUSES accept (exit 2) rather than treating the pair as uncovered-and-scaffolding it. So the predicate is not parity with VL-020 (which passes on both those states), and 'never mistaken for coverage' understates the actual behavior (accept aborts). This is fully self-disclosed in the ac-2 body's round-3/round-4 correction notes and stated as queued for ratification / an accepted deviation — flagged here only so the frozen-text-vs-code gap stays tracked until the AC wording is ratified; it is a safety-increasing (refuse-where-VL-020-would-pass) divergence, never a coverage-weakening one. (confidence 0.40) — The VL-020-parity deviation, re-slugged from the build round's judged-backstop-coverage-vs-vl020-predicate (accepted-deviation). Behavior is correct and deliberately STRICTER than VL-020 (adds for_kind==kind; catches at accept-time a misfile VL-011 reds at lint, one gate earlier). The frozen ac-2 'same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies' names the shared convention-path locus, not full parity; the body's round-3/round-4 corrections disclose it exactly. Frozen-text precision QUEUED FOR RATIFICATION (08-revision-notes at feature close). Honesty slice, not fix-class.

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation

(none)

## Not resurfaced

- **judged-backstop-coverage-vs-vl020-predicate** [not-resurfaced, was accepted-deviation]: ac-2's frozen wording asserts coverage is decided by 'the same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies (a DECODABLE obligation.md at the convention path)'. The code diverges: VL-020 (vl020.go:74-77) is existence-only — it keys existingObligations on d.Kind=='obligation' at the RelPath and never inspects for_kind (its own doc: 'Existence only ... does not re-validate that file's own content'). The backstop (acceptobligation.go:127-147 via evidence.ObligationKindAt, which decodes for_kind) additionally requires present && forKind==kind, and REFUSES accept (exit 2) in two states VL-020 treats as covered/passing: (1) a present-but-undecodable file at the convention path, and (2) a decodable obligation at the exact convention path whose for_kind disagrees with its filename (VL-020 passes; it is VL-011, not VL-020, that reds this). So the predicate is provably STRICTER than VL-020, not 'the same', and it introduces refusal outcomes ac-2's scaffold-missing/skip-covered/byte-untouched framing does not contemplate. This is no longer a silent divergence: the spec body's round-3 and round-4 corrections now name it exactly (naming judged-coverage-predicate-forkind-keying, retracting the earlier 'can only refuse where VL-020 would pass' and 'VL-020 reds it' claims), and the code comments disclose it — the residual gap is that the FROZEN ac-2 text still overclaims full predicate-parity and is only 'queued for ratification precision'. Impact is narrow (the stricter arms can only refuse safely, and end-to-end accept still converges via VL-011/VL-020 at lint), so this is disclosed-as-accepted rather than a correctness defect, but the implementation's intent is deliberately stricter than the literal AC and the two are not equivalent as ac-2 states. (confidence 0.32) — Behavior is correct and deliberately STRICTER than VL-020 (adds for_kind==kind), which is safer: it catches at accept-time a misfiled/inconsistent obligation that VL-020's existence-only check would pass and that VL-011 independently reds at lint — one gate earlier, never freezing a story with an absent or internally-inconsistent obligation. The body disclosure now names the divergence exactly (round-3 + round-4 corrections, judge-acknowledged: 'no longer a silent divergence ... disclosed-as-accepted rather than a correctness defect'; confidence fell 0.50->0.42->0.32 as it converged). The sole residual is that the FROZEN ac-2 letter ('the same decode-based predicate VL-020 itself applies') overclaims full parity when the backstop is stricter; frozen AC text cannot be edited in place (X-16), so its precision is QUEUED FOR RATIFICATION (08-revision-notes at feature close), like init-wizard's ENOTEMPTY/os.Rename corrections. Terminal disposition per the loop policy — a disclosed-accepted honesty slice, not a fix-class finding.
